goal: Implement the heavily gated first active real config write pilot, run the copied active-config rehearsal, and execute the live pilot only if every safety gate passes.

chosenPilot.rationale: An unused named bezier curve is the safest possible structured-family change: it preserves every existing record, appends exactly one single-line record, references nothing, and has zero behavioral effect even if the config were reloaded. The active config currently has zero hl.curve records.

copiedActiveConfigRehearsal.method: the real active config content was copied into a temp rehearsal root and the controlled executor ran the full round trip against the copy: byte-exact backup, single appended inert curve record, parser/projection reread verification, restore, restore verification

firstActiveConfigWritePilot.blockingEvidence: read-only `hyprctl getoption misc:disable_autoreload` returned `bool: false` (autoreload enabled, the Hyprland default). With autoreload enabled, any write to the active config file would make the running compositor reload it immediately - a live runtime mutation. Runtime mutation is not approved in this sprint, so the pilot's NoRuntimeMutationPlanned gate cannot pass.
firstActiveConfigWritePilot.preWriteHash: not recorded - pilot never reached the write step
firstActiveConfigWritePilot.backupPath: not created - pilot never reached the backup step
firstActiveConfigWritePilot.howToUnblock: either set misc:disable_autoreload=true in the Hyprland config (a user decision) or explicitly approve the one-shot runtime reload that autoreload would cause; then run the ignored test first_active_config_write_pilot_runs_only_when_every_gate_passes with HYPRLAND_SETTINGS_RUN_ACTIVE_PILOT=1 and HYPRLAND_SETTINGS_AUTORELOAD_DISABLED_CONFIRMED=true after re-verifying the option read-only

pilotExecutionPath.accidentalTriggerImpossible: the pilot is unreachable from UI, main, and write_flow; the normal test suite never runs it; it requires an explicit approval object with a typed confirmation phrase; and every gate must pass immediately before the write
pilotExecutionPath.writeSemantics: backup byte-exact to a path outside the active config area, verify backup readable, pre-write parser/projection reread with drift detection, one atomic write via temp-file rename, post-write parser/projection verification, restoration of the original bytes, byte-exact and parser/projection restore verification, receipt and audit record with FNV-1a content hashes

productionReadinessDecision: not production ready for active real config writes
nextRecommendedWork: Stop for explicit user decision: approve or reject a first active real config write pilot for structured families. Unblocking it requires either disabling compositor config autoreload (misc:disable_autoreload=true) or explicitly approving the runtime reload that the write would trigger.
gateHardening: the ignored pilot test now requires BOTH the operator env flag AND live-collected read-only autoreload evidence (collect_autoreload_evidence fails closed)
